description.p: An Armorer modifies armor to function almost like a second skin. The armor is enhanced to hone the Armorer's magic, unleash potent attacks, and generate a formidable defense.
You gain the following benefits.
Armor Training. You gain training with Heavy armor.
Tool Proficiency. You gain proficiency with Smith's Tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of Artisan's Tools of your choice.
Armor Crafting. When you craft nonmagical or magic armor, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.
When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Armorer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

As a Magic action while you have Smith's Tools in hand, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor. The armor continues to be Arcane Armor until you don another suit of armor or you die.
You gain the following benefits while wearing your Arcane Armor.
No Strength Requirement. If the armor normally has a Strength requirement, the Arcane Armor lacks this requirement for you.
Quick Don and Doff. You can don or doff the armor as a Utilize action. The armor can't be removed against your will.
Spellcasting Focus. You can use the Arcane Armor as a Spellcasting Focus for your Artificer spells.

sheet.description: Turn worn armor into Arcane Armor. It ignores Strength requirement, can be donned/doffed as a Utilize action, can't be removed against your will, and is a spellcasting focus.

description.p: You can customize your Arcane Armor. Choose Dreadnaught, Guardian, or Infiltrator. You can change the model whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest if you have Smith's Tools in hand.
Each model includes a special weapon. When you attack with that weapon, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, to the attack and damage rolls.

sheet.description: At the end of a Short or Long Rest, you can customize your Arcane Armor's model. Each Model includes a weapon, which uses {{Armorer:attack_bonus}} as bonus for attack and damage rolls.

description.p: You design your Arcane Armor for overwhelming physical presence. It gains the following features.

sheet.description: You design your Arcane Armor for overwhelming physical presence.

description.p: Your Arcane Armor includes a Force Demolisher. It counts as a Simple Melee weapon with Reach, deals 1d10 Force damage, and when you hit a creature at least one size smaller than you, you can push or pull it up to 10 feet.

sheet.description: Force Demolisher: Simple Melee weapon with Reach, 1d10 Force damage; on hit, push or pull a creature at least one size smaller than you up to 10 feet.
Force Demolisher: Simple Melee weapon with Reach, 2d6 Force damage; on hit, push or pull a creature at least one size smaller than you up to 10 feet.

description.p: As a Bonus Action, you can enlarge your armor for 1 minute. While enlarged, your Reach increases by 5 feet, and if you are smaller than Large, you become Large. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

sheet.description: For 1 minute, enlarge your armor: Reach +5 feet, you become Large.
For 1 minute, enlarge your armor: Reach +15 feet, you become Large od Huge. You gain ADV on STR check and Saves.

description.p: You configure your Arcane Armor to protect allies and control the battlefield. It gains the following features.

sheet.description: You configure your Arcane Armor to protect allies and control the battlefield.

description.p: Your Arcane Armor includes a Thunder Pulse. It counts as a Simple Melee weapon and deals 1d8 Thunder damage. A creature hit by it has Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until your next turn.

sheet.description: Thunder Pulse: Simple Melee weapon, 1d8 Thunder damage; a creature hit has Disadvantage on attacks against targets other than you until your next turn.

Thunder Pulse: Simple Melee weapon, 1d10 Thunder damage; a creature hit has Disadvantage on attacks against targets other than you until your next turn. Moreover, if a Huge or smaller creature ends its turn wihtin 30ft of you, you can take a Reaction to force it to make a STR check DC{{spellcasting:dc}}. On a fail you pull the creature up to 25ft towards you. If you pull the target whitin 5f of you, you can make a melle attack as part of the Reaciton. You can take this reaction {{guardian reaction}} times per Long Rest.

description.p: While Bloodied, you can take a Bonus Action to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Artificer level. You lose these Temporary Hit Points if you doff the armor.

sheet.description: While Bloodied, gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Artificer level. You lose these Temporary Hit Points if you doff the armor.

description.p: You adapt your Arcane Armor for speed and subtlety. It gains the following features.

sheet.description: You adapt your Arcane Armor for speed and subtlety.

description.p: Your Arcane Armor includes a Lightning Launcher. It counts as a Simple Ranged weapon with a range of 90/300, deals 1d6 Lightning damage, and once per turn can deal an extra 1d6 Lightning damage on a hit.

sheet.description: Lightning Launcher: Simple Ranged weapon (90/300), 1d6 Lightning damage, and once per turn it deals an extra 1d6 Lightning damage on a hit.

Lightning Launcher: Simple Ranged weapon (90/300), 2d6 Lightning damage, and once per turn it deals an extra 1d6 Lightning damage on a hit. The damaged target glimmers in 5ft Dim light and has Disadvantage to attack you until the start of your next turn. Moreover, as a Bonus Action you gain {{infiltrator speed}}ft Flying speed until the end of the turn. You can take this Bonus action {{infiltrator flight}} times per Long Rest.

description.p: Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
You have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. If the armor would normally impose Disadvantage on those checks, the Advantage and Disadvantage cancel as normal.

sheet.description: You have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks; if the armor would normally impose Disadvantage, they cancel as normal.

description.p: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
You gain the following benefits.

Armor Replication. You learn an additional plan for your Replicate Magic Item feature, and it must be in the Armor category. If you replace that plan, you must replace it with another Armor plan. In addition, you can create an additional item with that feature, and the item must also be in the Armor category.
Improved Arsenal. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with the special weapon of your Arcane Armor model.

select.name: Magic Item Plans: Armorer
select.supports: Magic Item Plan, EFA Version, Armor

description.p: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model.
